title: Cow 1
description: Cow (Clone-on-Write) is a smart pointer that can hold either borrowed or owned data, cloning only when mutation is needed.
instructions: Use Cow to avoid unnecessary allocations.
hints: Cow::from() can take either a reference or an owned value.
to_mut() returns a mutable reference, cloning the data if it was borrowed.
This code should compile. Study how Cow avoids unnecessary clones.
explanation: Cow<T> (Clone-on-Write) holds either a reference (`Borrowed`) or an owned value (`Owned`). The `to_mut()` method returns `&mut T` - if the data is borrowed, it clones it first. If the data is already owned, no clone is needed. This optimization pattern is useful when you might or might not need to modify data.
